destinations.name: Badrinath Temple
destinations.description: Badrinath Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu.
destinations.history: Mentioned in ancient texts like the Bhagavata Purana.
destinations.religious_significance: One of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites.
destinations.architecture: North Indian style architecture with traditional Garhwali wooden elements.

nearby_attractions.name: Mana Village
nearby_attractions.description: Last Indian village near the border.

nearby_attractions.name: Vasudhara Falls
nearby_attractions.description: A beautiful waterfall near Mana village.

nearby_attractions.name: Tapt Kund
nearby_attractions.description: Natural hot water spring.

nearby_attractions.name: Neelkanth Peak
nearby_attractions.description: A snowy peak forming the backdrop of Badrinath.

nearby_attractions.name: Charanpaduka
nearby_attractions.description: A rock bearing the footprints of Lord Vishnu.

nearby_attractions.name: Mata Murti Temple
nearby_attractions.description: Temple dedicated to the mother of Nara and Narayana.

nearby_attractions.name: Vyas Gufa
nearby_attractions.description: Ancient cave where sage Vyasa composed the Mahabharata.

nearby_attractions.name: Ganesh Gufa
nearby_attractions.description: Cave where Lord Ganesha wrote the Mahabharata.

nearby_attractions.name: Saraswati River Origin
nearby_attractions.description: The origin point of the mythical Saraswati river.

nearby_attractions.name: Valley of Flowers
nearby_attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage site known for alpine flowers.

routes.road_condition: Hilly terrain, well-maintained but prone to landslides in monsoon

destinations.name: Golden Temple
destinations.description: The Golden Temple is the preeminent spiritual site of Sikhism.
destinations.history: Built by the fifth Sikh Guru, Guru Arjan, around the holy sarovar.
destinations.religious_significance: The holiest Gurdwara and the most important pilgrimage site of Sikhism.
destinations.architecture: A blend of Hindu Rajput and Islamic Mughal styles with gold-foil-covered upper floors.
destinations.founder: Guru Ram Das and Guru Arjan
destinations.dress_code: Head covered, bare feet, modest clothing
destinations.travel_tips: Visit during early morning for the Palki Sahib ceremony. Partake in the Langar.

nearby_attractions.name: Jallianwala Bagh
nearby_attractions.description: Historic garden and memorial of national importance.

nearby_attractions.name: Wagah Border
nearby_attractions.description: Border crossing between India and Pakistan known for the beating retreat ceremony.

nearby_attractions.name: Partition Museum
nearby_attractions.description: Museum detailing the history of the 1947 partition of India.

nearby_attractions.name: Akal Takht
nearby_attractions.description: The highest seat of earthly authority of the Khalsa.

nearby_attractions.name: Gurudwara Baba Atal Rai
nearby_attractions.description: A nine-story octagonal tower near the Golden Temple.

nearby_attractions.name: Durgiana Temple
nearby_attractions.description: A premier Hindu temple of Punjab situated in the city of Amritsar.

nearby_attractions.name: Gobindgarh Fort
nearby_attractions.description: Historic military fort built in the 18th century.

nearby_attractions.name: Sadda Pind
nearby_attractions.description: A Punjabi culture living village museum.

nearby_attractions.name: Ram Bagh Gardens
nearby_attractions.description: Beautiful gardens containing the summer palace of Maharaja Ranjit Singh.

nearby_attractions.name: Khalsa College
nearby_attractions.description: Historic educational institution with stunning Indo-Saracenic architecture.

flight_information.arrival_airport: Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport (ATQ)

destinations.name: Palitana Temples
destinations.description: The Palitana temples on Shatrunjaya hill form the largest cluster of Jain temples.
destinations.history: Over 900 temples built over a period of 900 years starting from the 11th century.
destinations.religious_significance: Considered the most sacred pilgrimage site by the Svetambara Jain community.
destinations.architecture: Māru-Gurjara architecture featuring intricate marble carvings.
destinations.dress_code: Strict traditional modest clothing; leather items prohibited
destinations.travel_tips: Involves climbing 3,500+ steps. Start early morning. Carry water.

nearby_attractions.name: Vishal Jain Museum
nearby_attractions.description: Museum displaying Jain artifacts and history.

nearby_attractions.name: Talaja Caves
nearby_attractions.description: Ancient Buddhist and Jain rock-cut caves.

nearby_attractions.name: Bhavnagar Blackbuck National Park
nearby_attractions.description: A unique ecosystem for blackbucks.

nearby_attractions.name: Gopinath Beach
nearby_attractions.description: Scenic beach near Bhavnagar.

nearby_attractions.name: Khodiyar Mata Temple
nearby_attractions.description: Popular temple set amidst a lake.

nearby_attractions.name: Gaurishankar Lake
nearby_attractions.description: Beautiful lake ideal for evening walks in Bhavnagar.

nearby_attractions.name: Takhteshwar Temple
nearby_attractions.description: Historic temple situated on a hillock in Bhavnagar.

nearby_attractions.name: Victoria Park
nearby_attractions.description: A protected forest area in Bhavnagar.

nearby_attractions.name: Alang Ship Recycling Yard
nearby_attractions.description: The world's largest ship recycling yard.

nearby_attractions.name: Nilambag Palace
nearby_attractions.description: A grand heritage palace in Bhavnagar.

routes.road_condition: Good highways for most of the journey

train_information.train_name: Delhi Sarai Rohilla - Bhavnagar Terminus Link Express

destinations.name: Mahabodhi Temple
destinations.description: An ancient, but much rebuilt and restored, Buddhist temple marking the location where the Buddha is said to have attained enlightenment.
destinations.history: Originally built by Emperor Ashoka in the 3rd century BCE.
destinations.religious_significance: The most sacred site in Buddhism, representing the site of Gautama Buddha's enlightenment.
destinations.architecture: Brick architecture with a central tower rising 55 meters.
destinations.travel_tips: Leave electronic devices outside; only cameras are allowed with a fee.

nearby_attractions.name: Bodhi Tree
nearby_attractions.description: The sacred fig tree under which Buddha attained enlightenment.

nearby_attractions.name: Great Buddha Statue
nearby_attractions.description: 80-foot tall statue of Buddha in meditation pose.

nearby_attractions.name: Thai Monastery
nearby_attractions.description: Beautiful Thai-style architecture with sloping curved roofs.

nearby_attractions.name: Japanese Temple
nearby_attractions.description: Temple with traditional Japanese architecture.

nearby_attractions.name: Sujata Stupa
nearby_attractions.description: Stupa dedicated to the milkmaid Sujata.

nearby_attractions.name: Dungeshwari Cave Temples
nearby_attractions.description: Caves where Buddha meditated before coming to Bodh Gaya.

nearby_attractions.name: Vishnupad Temple
nearby_attractions.description: Ancient Hindu temple in Gaya dedicated to Lord Vishnu.

nearby_attractions.name: Archaeological Society of India Museum
nearby_attractions.description: Museum holding a collection of Buddhist and Hindu relics.

nearby_attractions.name: Muchalinda Lake
nearby_attractions.description: Sacred lake associated with the snake king Muchalinda.

nearby_attractions.name: Root Institute
nearby_attractions.description: A Tibetan Buddhist center offering meditation courses.

routes.road_condition: Good highways mostly via Agra-Lucknow Expressway

destinations.name: Udvada Atash Behram
destinations.description: The oldest continuously burning fire temple in India, housing the sacred Iranshah fire.
destinations.history: The sacred fire was brought from Iran to India in the 8th century.
destinations.religious_significance: The most sacred of the Zoroastrian fire temples in India.
destinations.main_deity_or_revered_person: Ahura Mazda (worshipped via the sacred fire)
destinations.travel_tips: Only Parsis and Zoroastrians are allowed inside the main sanctum. Non-Zoroastrians can view from outside.

nearby_attractions.name: Zoroastrian Heritage Museum
nearby_attractions.description: Museum detailing Parsi history and culture in India.

nearby_attractions.name: Udvada Beach
nearby_attractions.description: Quiet, black sand beach bordering the Arabian Sea.

nearby_attractions.name: Daman Fort
nearby_attractions.description: Historic Portuguese fort in nearby Daman.

nearby_attractions.name: Jampore Beach
nearby_attractions.description: Popular beach in Daman.

nearby_attractions.name: Devka Beach
nearby_attractions.description: Rocky beach known for amusement parks.

nearby_attractions.name: Tithal Beach
nearby_attractions.description: Famous black sand beach near Valsad.

nearby_attractions.name: BAPS Swaminarayan Temple
nearby_attractions.description: Large ornate Hindu temple in Navsari.

nearby_attractions.name: Parnera Hill
nearby_attractions.description: Hill featuring a fort and multiple temples.

nearby_attractions.name: Wilson Hills
nearby_attractions.description: Scenic hill station in the Dharampur region.

nearby_attractions.name: Sajan Nature Club
nearby_attractions.description: Nature retreat and adventure park.
